title: Gate verdict
description: One gate execution's structured result for one candidate (or one question of a candidate). Every gate — mechanical script or prompt-executed agent — emits exactly this shape, one JSON object per line in the batch's verdicts.jsonl. Aggregation is purely mechanical over these records.

target.description: 'passage' for passage-level gates (M-*, G-SPRAK, G-ENG, G-REGISTER); 'q:<q_index>' for per-question gates (G-KEY, G-STEM, G-DISTRACTOR).

verdict.description: pass = no disqualifying finding. kill = disqualifying finding (semantics per gate; see runbook aggregation). flag = survives but the finding must appear in the adjudication package.

findings.description: Empty array on a clean pass. Every kill or flag MUST carry at least one finding with a verbatim quote.

quote.description: Verbatim excerpt from the candidate that the finding is about. Never paraphrased — aggregation and adjudication anchor on it.

note.description: Why this is a defect, one or two sentences. English.

solver_answer.description: G-KEY only: the answer the blind solver committed to before seeing the key.

vote.description: For gates run as N independent votes (G-KEY, G-SPRAK, G-ENG): which vote this record is (1..N). Omit for single-run gates.

executed_by.description: Free-form executor id: script name+version for mechanical gates, agent/model tag for prompt gates.
